metadata.title: Dataset for Biotin- and Chloroalkane-Modified Nucleotides for Dual Display of Proteins on DNA through Streptavidin and Halotag Binding
metadata.publisher: Ústav organické chemie a biochemie AV ČR

additional_descriptions.description: We designed and synthesized 2'-deoxyribonucleoside triphosphates (dNTPs) bearing chorohexyl and biotin modifications attached to position 7 of 7-deazaadenine or to position 5 of cytosine. The modified dNTPs served as substrates for KOD XL DNA polymerase in enzymatic construction of DNA bearing either one or combination of the two modifications. The chlorohexyl-linked DNA underwent covalent reactions with halotag protein, while the biotin-linked DNA formed stable non-covalent complexes with monomeric streptavidin. The dually modified DNA bearing both modifications at different distances apart from each other were used for sequence-specific spatially defined display of both halotag and streptavidin proteins. The dually modified DNA bearing both modifications successfully displayed the two proteins on DNA scaffold.

The data was created as a result of the author's original research. For the Organic chemistry dataset general protocols for cross-coupling reactions and triphosphorylation reactions were used. For the Biochemistry dataset Single Nucleotide Incorporation (SNI), Primer Extension (PEX) reactions, TdT mediated incorporation of modified nucleotides, and DNA-protein cross-linking and DNA-protein complex formation reactions were used. For the Binding assay dataset DNA-protein complex formation reaction was used. For the Graphics dataset sequences from commercial supplier or Protein Data Bank, and SMILES for ligands were used. For the Flow cytometry dataset protocol for TUNEL assay was used, followed by Flow cytometry detection.

Dataset contains raw NMR data for all synthetized compounds (NMR folder; zip file size: 130.12 MB). 
Dataset contains raw MALDI-TOF and LC-ESI-MS data for all synthetized oligonucleotides (Mass_oligonucleotides folder; zip file size: 4.76 MB).
Dataset contains raw images (.tif) of all gels for Single Nucleotide Incorporation, Primer Extension reactions, TdT incorporation of modified nucleotides, and DNA-proteins reactions (Gels folder; zip file size: 311.81 MB).
Dataset contains Flow Cytometry raw data (.fcs) of Flow Cytometry analysis after TUNEL assay; raw data (.xlsx) of Fluorescent Nickel-plate binding assay (Characterization interaction biotin-modified DNA + mSA folder; zip file size: 11.08 MB).
Dataset contains raw data (.pdb, .pse) for generation of graphics of DNA-protein assemblies (Graphics DNA-protein assemblies folder; zip file size: 1.84 MB).

metadata.title: J. Heyrovsky Institute Community
Data for 'ICARION: A Modular Framework for Ion Trajectory Simulation in Electric Fields and Neutral Gas Environments'
metadata.publisher: Ústav fyzikální chemie J. Heyrovského AV ČR

additional_descriptions.description:
This dataset contains the data and workflow files associated with the validation studies presented in the ICARION article. ICARION is a simulation framework for ion trajectory simulations in ion mobility spectrometry and mass spectrometry. The dataset includes all configuration files required to reproduce the ICARION simulations for collisional thermalization, bimolecular ion–molecule reaction validation, ion mobility benchmarking, coupled IMS–quadrupole simulations, and performance benchmarking.

The dataset provides the raw simulation outputs generated by ICARION as HDF5 files, the preprocessing scripts used to generate configuration files and execute batch simulations, and the postprocessing scripts used for data analysis. Derived results underlying the figures, tables, and benchmark comparisons presented in the ICARION article are also included.

In addition, the dataset includes the corresponding configuration files, preprocessing and postprocessing files, raw output data where applicable, and derived results from benchmark simulations performed with IDSimF and SIMION.

Furthermore, it includes input and output files from ORCA geometry optimizations, as well as input and output files for collision cross section calculations performed with MobCal-MPI 2.0.

Together, these files provide the information required to reproduce the simulation workflows and regenerate the main validation results reported in the associated article.

The following software versions were used: 
ICARION v1.0.1; 
ORCA 6.1.0; 
SIMION 8.1.1.32; 
IDSimF v1.1; 
MobCal-MPI 2.0.3

The dataset includes the following directories: 

"thermalization.zip" includes all files related to collisional thermalization simulations conducted with the ICARION framework. This includes roughly 95 GB of input files (.json), generated trajectory files (.h5) and associated logs (.log), extracted heating curves and velocity distributions (.csv), and pre- and postprocessing scripts for automated configuration file generation, batch simulation, and data analysis (.py). This directory also includes control simulations with matched collision areas for the EHSS and HSS simulations, as well as for estimating statistical uncertainty, using the same underlying file formats. 

"reaction_kinetics.zip" includes all files related to bimolecular reaction kinetics simulations conducted with the ICARION framework. This includes roughly 350 MB of input files (.json), generated trajectory files (.h5) and associated logs (.log), extracted ion counts (.csv), and pre- and postprocessing scripts for automated configuration file generation, batch simulation, and data analysis (.py). 

"ims.zip" includes all files related to ion mobility simulations in a drift cell conducted with the ICARION framework. This includes roughly 107 GB of input files (.json), generated trajectory files (.h5) and associated logs (.log), extracted arrival time distributions and summarized mobility data (.csv), and pre- and postprocessing scripts for automated configuration file generation, batch simulation, and data analysis (.py). This directory also includes control simulations with matched collision areas for the EHSS and HSS simulations, as well as for estimating statistical uncertainty, using the same underlying file formats. 

"multi_domain.zip" includes all files related to IMS-quadrupole simulations conducted with the ICARION framework. This includes roughly 4 GB of input files (.json), generated trajectory files (.h5) and associated logs (.log), extracted arrival time distributions and transmission ratios (.csv), and pre- and postprocessing scripts for automated configuration file generation, batch simulation, and data analysis (.py). 

"benchmark.zip" includes all files related to performance benchmark simulations conducted with the ICARION framework. This includes roughly 270 MB of input files (.json), generated trajectory files (.h5) and associated logs (.log), extracted performance stats (.csv, .json), associated thread scaling plots (.png), and pre- and postprocessing scripts for automated configuration file generation, batch simulation, and data analysis (.py). 

"ORCA.zip" includes all files related to ORCA simulations. This includes roughly 3 MB of input files (.inp, .xyz) and generated output files (.out). 

"SIMION.zip" includes all files related to SIMION simulations. This includes roughly 30 MB of input files (.GEM, .lua, .fly2, .rec), generated output logs (.txt), plots of arrival time distributions (.png), a summary of extracted mobilities (.csv), and postprocessing scripts for automated data analysis (.py). 

"IDSimF.zip" includes all files related to IDSimF simulations. This includes roughly 7 GB of input files (.json, .conf), generated trajectory files (.h5), plots of arrival time distributions (.png), summaries of extracted mobilities (.csv), and pre- and postprocessing scripts for automated configuration file generation, batch simulation, and data analysis (.py). 

"MobCal-MPI.zip" includes all files related to MobCal simulations. This includes roughly 2 MB of input files (.mfj), output files (.mout), and extracted mobility data (.csv).

metadata.title: J. Heyrovsky Institute Community
Computational and experimental data for "Modeling the uptake of small oxidized organic molecules by gas-phase hydrated nitric acid clusters"
metadata.publisher: Ústav fyzikální chemie J. Heyrovského AV ČR

additional_descriptions.description: The uploaded files contain initial conditions and other parameters to perform classical molecular dynamics simulations of uptake of methanol, formaldehyde, acetaldehyde, formic and acetic acid on aqueous nitric acid clusters. These simulations complemented  experimental mass spectra available as a spreadsheet. Results of the joint project have implications for modelling of new particle formation in the atmosphere.

Classical molecular dynamics simulations with empirical force field in Gromacs 2020.3 programm package. The trajectory analysis was performed using the Gromacs utilities and home-made scripts. In the experiment, cluster beam was formed in supersonic expansion. Beam was then ionized by electron ionization  mass spectra of ionized beam were recorded using reflectron time-of-flight mass spectrometer.

The Pickup_SI_data.zip contains 10 folders: AAC (data for acetic acid), AAL (data for acetaldehyde), FAC (data for formic acid), FAL (data for formaldehyde) MEO (data for methanol), AW6 (data for (H3O+∙NO3 − )∙(H2O)5 cluster) and AW20 (data for (H3O+∙NO3 − )∙(H2O)19 cluster). Each folder contains concatenated structures (coordinates and velocities) in a .gro file format needed to launch the simulations and force field parameters in .itp format. The file Data4Mass_spectra_Fig11_S10_S11_S12.xlsx lists the mass spectra shown in Fig. 11, S10, S11 and S12 in the form as Excell spreadsheet.

metadata.title: J. Heyrovsky Institute Community
Dataset: Adsorption and Translocation of Arginine-Rich Peptides at Phosphatidylcholine Membranes
metadata.publisher: Ústav fyzikální chemie J. Heyrovského AV ČR

additional_descriptions.description: On this dataset, we demonstrate that the commonly used chelating agent EDTA strongly influences peptide–membrane interactions in zwitterionic POPC membranes. More specifically, the first datasheet presents our results from leakage assays on giant unilamellar vesicles (GUVs), showing that EDTA suppresses R9-induced membrane permeabilization and largely eliminates the characteristic differences between R9 and lysine-rich K9 peptides. The suppressing effect is concentration-dependent and independent of fluorescent labelling. 2) The second datasheet presents our results from Fluorescence Cross-correlation Spectroscopy (FCCS) to characterize the binding of R9 and K9 peptides to zwitterionic membranes, showing that EDTA suppresses binding of R9 to the membrane, thus lowering the efficiency of R9 translocation across zwitterionic membranes.

The methodology includes permeabilization experiments with giant liposomes monitored by confocal microscopy, and binding experiments of fluorescently labeled peptides to liposome membranes studied using cross-correlation fluorescence microscopy according to the methodology described in the article's supporting information.

Fluorescence microscopy to monitor membrane permeabilization was performed using an Olympus FluoView 1000 MPE system equipped with a PicoQuant laser scanning module and diode laser excitation at 470 and 640 nm and additional 532 nm laser. Images were acquired at resolutions of 512 × 512 or 1024 × 1024 pixels with dwell times of 4–8 µs. Image analysis was performed using ImageJ, and leakage was quantified for individual vesicles using fluorescence intensity ratios. Each experiment was repeated independently (at least three times), and altogether more than 500 vesicles were analyzed per each condition.

FCCS measurements were performed using a custom-built setup based on an Olympus IX71 inverted microscope. This configuration included a 635 nm dichroic mirror (Chroma, USA), a water immersion objective (UPLSAPO 60×, Olympus, Japan), a 3D sample scanning stage (PI Mars XYZ NanoPositioner, PI, Germany), and a 50 μm pinhole. Fluorescence detection was achieved using two emission filters (697/58 nm and 525/50 nm; Chroma, USA) and a pair of single-photon avalanche diodes (PDM, MPD, Italy). Excitation was provided by two pulsed lasers: 470 nm (LDHP-C-470, PicoQuant, Germany) and 635 nm (LDH-D-C-635, PicoQuant, Germany), each operating at a repetition rate of 10 MHz in an alternating mode. Laser power was kept around 10 μW for both channels. Each sample was measured for a minimum of 2 minutes and a maximum of 5 minutes.

metadata.title: Data průměrné sluneční póry
metadata.publisher: Univerzita Karlova

additional_titles.title: Data of an average solar pore

additional_descriptions.description: Dataset containing results of ensemble averaging performed on a set of 13 ("broad" set) and 11 ("narrow" set) solar pores. Videos and FITS files in continuum intensity (Ic), line-of-sight magnetograms (M), vector magnetic intensities (Bp, Bt, Br), radial velocities (V), and perturbations of travel times of helioseismic waves (TT) record the evolution of the average solar pore, with time normalized to hold the emergent phase in the interval [0; 1]. Analyses of the average solar pore, most importantly cross-sections and azimuthal averages, are available as plots and FITS files.

Two sets, a "narrow" set of 11 and a "broad" set of 13, solar pores were selected based on their magnetic classification and manual validation. Observations of these solar pores were downloaded from the SDO/HMI archive. Pore positions were tracked using a circle-fitting algorithm and centre-of-mass in continuum intensity. Based on the tracking data, magnetic flux through each pore was integrated and its evolution in time used to normalize the recordings in time. The growth phase of the pore was fitted using a linear function and the temporal axis of the recording was re-normalized such that the growth phase begins at the start of emergence at normalized time 0 and ends at the maximum flux at normalized time 1. Recordings of individual pores were aligned in both space and time, overlaid and averaged. This was done for each of the following quantites: continuum intensity, line-of-sight magnetogram, vector magnetic intensity, radial velocity, and perturbations of helioseismic travel times (in out-in, east-west, and south-north geometries and the f and p1 through p4 modes). The results were then analyzed using a small set of simple tools, most importantly the evolution of pore cross-section and azimuthal average in time.

A TAR archive for each physical quantity and each set contains a sequence of (2D) images in FITS format (https://fits.gsfc.nasa.gov/fits_home.html) with the results of ensemble averaging. Each FITS file contains the averaged image, the Poisson noise and the standard deviations. Coordinate systems and physical units are described in the CRPIXn, CDELTn, and BUNIT headers. Each FITS file also contains the TIMENORM header containing the normalized time of the image.

metadata.title: J. Heyrovsky Institute Community
The computational data for article "Electrochemical transistor based on PEDOT: The influence of anionic dopant containing carbon nanotubes and SO3 groups"
metadata.publisher: Ústav fyzikální chemie J. Heyrovského AV ČR

additional_descriptions.description: The dataset titled "Computational data for the article 'Electrochemical transistor based on PEDOT: The influence of anionic dopants containing carbon nanotubes and SO3 groups'" contains the xyz coordinates of the systems calculated for this article. The summary of the dataset is provided in the file Summary.txt.
The structures included in this dataset were optimized either using the B3LYP density functional method or the semiempirical PM6-D3H4 method.
XYZ coordinates are in Å. For computational tasks, we used MOPAC 2016 or G16/G09 programs.

metadata.title: J. Heyrovsky Institute Community
Data pro přepínání host-hostitelského typu
metadata.publisher: Ústav fyzikální chemie J. Heyrovského AV ČR

additional_titles.title: Data for host guest switching

additional_descriptions.description: Data for chemical-to-electrochemical signal transduction is reported based on the tandem switching of a bis-acridinium macrocycle and a proflavine derivative. Molecular recognition triggers a simultaneous guest−host transformation converting the acridinium units into their redox-inactive acridane counterparts. This unusual switching mechanism arises from the multiresponsive nature of the acridinium receptor, complementary acid−base properties of the host and guest, and stabilization of a water-assisted transition state within the macrocyclic cavity. When grafted onto a gold surface, the bis-acridinium macrocycle enables the selective electrochemical detection of proflavine from a complex mixture of ten polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons.
Cyclic voltammetry of different sensoring layers based on the self-assembly.

Data set contains a series of cyclic voltammograms that were measured using Metrohm Autolab instrumentation on the samples containing self-assembled monolayers at different rates and compositions of the analyzed solution. Individual folders contain also Origin files of the measured cyclic voltammograms.

metadata.title: J. Heyrovsky Institute Community
Data for Modulating the Bipolaronic Units in the Graphene/Polyaniline Heterostructure by Metal Ion Doping
metadata.publisher: Ústav fyzikální chemie J. Heyrovského AV ČR

additional_descriptions.description: This dataset supports the published article "Modulating the Bipolaronic Units in the Graphene/Polyaniline Heterostructure by Metal Ion Doping", ACS Applied Polymer Materials, 2026, DOI: 10.1021/acsapm.6c00640 The article provides fundamental explanation of the charge-carrier reorganization in polyaniline after the incorporation of cobalt(II) ions in the graphene/polyaniline heterostructure.

The graphene/polyaniline heterostructure is prepared by oxidative polymerization of aniline hydrochloride on the surface of CVD-graphene functionalized by sulfonic groups. Cobalt(II) ions are incorporated by an exposure to aqueous solution of cobalt(II) chloride. The graphene/polyaniline+CoCl2 heterostructure is characterized by various methods: The chemical structure and the oxidation state and nature of charge carriers in cobalt(II) modified polyaniline by Raman and XPS spectroscopies. The Raman spectroelectrochemistry is employed to obtain information on the reversibility of redox transitions in polyaniline and charge-carrier injection into graphene. The thickness is determined by XPS.

The data is provided in text files. Some datasets are provided in their respective software formats, too. All datasets are located in folders named after the figures (e.g., Figure1_Raman, Figure2_Raman-SEC, etc.).

metadata.title: J. Heyrovsky Institute Community
Data for Robust Phonon Engineering and Symmetry-selective Lattice Dynamics in CrSBr(1−x)Cl(x)
metadata.publisher: Eötvös Loránd Tudományegyetem

additional_descriptions.description: This dataset supports the published article "Robust Phonon Engineering and Symmetry-selective Lattice Dynamics in CrSBr(1−x)Cl(x.)" In this work, we show how atomic substitution provides a controlled route to engineer lattice dynamics in low-symmetry two-dimensional materials. Here, we investigate the evolution of phonon characteristics in CrSBr1−xClx (0 ≤ x ≤∼ 0.5) upon partial substitution of Br with Cl atoms and show that progressive Cl substitution of Br induces systematic shifts of parent CrSBr out-of-plane Ag phonon modes and activates additional Raman features. Complementary strain-dependent calculations reveal that anisotropic lattice compression plays a key role in renormalizing Cr–S dominated phonons. Under near-resonant excitation, stimulated Raman scattering-like amplification remains observable with increasing Cl content, highlighting the resilience of anisotropic electron–phonon coupling in this system.
Sample preparation, including the crystal synthesis and characterization using optical microscopy and energy-dispersive X-ray spectroscopy (EDS). Raman spectroscopy. Density functional theory (DFT).

The data is provided in Excel spreadsheets or text files. All datasets are accompanied by the corresponding figures and are located in folders named after the figures (eg, Figure 1, Figure 2, etc.). This 3 MB file contains raw data for 5 figures from the main manuscript and for 5 supporting figures.

metadata.title: Data for "Ozonization for MALDI-2 MS imaging of carbon–carbon double bond positional isomers of phosphatidylethanolamines in biological tissues"
metadata.publisher: Masarykova univerzita

additional_descriptions.description: This dataset contains MALDI(-2) MS and MS/MS spectra and images of tissues treated with ozone recorded on timsTOF fleX MALDI-2 instrument (Bruker Daltonics GmbH) in the original file format distributed into folders named according to the figures in the linked publication. Exceptions are Figure S6 containing ESI MS/MS spectra from Q-Exactive (Thermo Fisher Scientific) in .raw format and Figure S10 contains data converted to .sbd file format (SCiLS base data) used by SCiLS Lab software (Bruker Daltonics GmbH) to reduce data size. Figure 1 contains MALDI-2 MS and MALDI-2 MS2 of PE 34:1 ozonide recorded from mouse brain. Figure 2 contains MALDI-2 MS2I of PE 36:1 isomers in the low-grade adenocarcinoma tissue. Figure 3 two MALDI-2 MS2I of PE 34:1 and PE 36:1 isomers in prenatal embryo, Figure 4 MALDI-2 MS2I of PC 34:1 isomers in mouse pup. The remaining data covers the MALDI MS and MSI experiments is provided in the Supporting Information.

Sections of biological tissues (6 -20 μm thick) were prepared using a cryomicrotome  at −20 °C and thaw-mounted on standard non-conductive microscope glass slides. Tissues were desalted by immersion into a cold (6 °C) 50 mmol L−1 aqueous solution of ammonium acetate three times for 5 s. The ozonization was carried out in a 500 mL glass flask with the glass slide positioned at the bottom. The ozone-enriched gas supplied by an ozone generator at a flow rate of 0.130 L min−1 was introduced to initiate the reaction. Ozone production was ∼2.76 g per minute (calculated based on the manufacturer's information). Afterwards, the DHB matrix was applied to the tissue sections by sublimation using a home-built apparatus with an evacuated chamber. In brief, 1.5 mL of a 20 mg mL−1 DHB solution in acetone was loaded into a preheated brass reservoir, and the solvent was allowed to evaporate. The sublimation proceeded for 15 min at ∼5 × 10−3 mbar and the matrix reservoir temperature of 130 °C. The matrix deposition resulted in a coverage of 330 ± 20 μg cm−2, calculated by weighing the glass slide before and after sublimation. Most of the MS and MSI measurements were conducted using a prototype timsTOF fleX MALDI-2 mass spectrometer (Bruker Daltonics) in the positive ion mode with the activated high sensitivity detection and the ion detection range set to m/z 300–1000 and m/z 400–800 for full-scan MALDI(-2) MS and MS2 measurements, respectively. The mass resolving power of the instrument was 40,000 (FWHM) in the phospholipid mass range, and mass accuracy was ≤2 ppm with external calibration. In MALDI-2 MS2 experiments, CID with nitrogen as the collision gas was employed. The optimal collision energy (CE) was set to 20 eV, the collision cell RF voltage to 2000 V. The MSI data were evaluated and visualized using SCiLS Lab MVS (vs 2023a, Bruker Daltonics). The ESI MS data were recorded on a Q-Exactive mass spectrometer (Thermo Scientific) with a mass resolving power of 140,000.

metadata.title: Dataset for Knowledge and practices regarding oral hygiene among school children: cross-sectional study
metadata.publisher: Jihočeská univerzita v Českých Budějovicích

additional_descriptions.description: The file in ".xlsx" format contains 28 numerical variables as a result of a questionnaire survey regarding the oral hygiene habits in 1333 respondents, as well as a codebook to present the full variable names and the connection of the numerical codes to the respective category labels.

A quantitative research design was chosen for the research study. It was a multicenter cross-sectional study. The questionnaire "Knowledge and practice of oral hygiene" (Parveen et al., 2022) was used. The research sample consisted of children aged 9 and 10.  It was a quota selection. A total of 1,333 children were included in the research study.

metadata.title: Databáze TLS mračen bodů, 3D modelů a virtuálních ALS dat pro středoevropské stromy
metadata.publisher: Ústav výzkumu globální změny AV ČR

additional_titles.title: Database of TLS Point Clouds, 3D Models, and Virtual ALS for Central European Trees

additional_descriptions.description:
This dataset provides a comprehensive, multi-scale structural library for dominant Central European tree species, designed primarily to facilitate the training and validation of machine learning algorithms in forest remote sensing. The collection is structured into two main scales: individual trees and forest plots.

At the individual tree level, the repository contains 3,618 detailed TLS point clouds with semantically separated wood and foliage components. Fully reconstructed 3D representations are provided for a selected subset, combining QSMs for woody architecture with procedurally generated 3D foliage meshes.

At the plot level, the dataset includes approximately 15,000 synthetic 50 × 50 m ALS scenes. These virtual plots were spatially assembled using the Forest Factory growth model and subsequently simulated via the Helios++ radiative transfer model. The simulation parameters strictly mimic real-world flight configurations of the CzechGlobe airborne LiDAR system, providing data at both standard (8p) and high-density (16p) resolutions.

This repository offers a robust, pre-annotated ground-truth baseline for developing advanced retrieval algorithms for forest structural properties, such as DBH or biomass, overcoming the logistical limitations of field data collection.

This dataset accompanies the manuscript "Dataset of Individual Tree Point Clouds, 3D Representations, and Virtual Airborne Laser Scanning Scenes for Central European Tree Species". It contains individual tree TLS point clouds, semantically separated 3D representations combining wood QSMs and foliage meshes, and procedurally generated synthetic ALS scenes. The foundational TLS data encompass 3,618 individual trees representing dominant Central European species. These structural templates were dynamically assembled into approximately 15,000 virtual 50 × 50 m spatial grids using the Forest Factory growth model. Multi-resolution ALS point clouds were subsequently simulated from these scenes via the Helios++ radiative transfer model. The simulations were explicitly parameterised to replicate the Riegl LMS Q-780 sensor and operational flight configurations of the CzechGlobe airborne LiDAR system.

metadata.title: Analýza a hodnocení komplexní hybridní technologie pro eliminaci především léčiv z odpadních vod produkovaných zdravotnictvím
metadata.publisher: Technická univerzita v Liberci

additional_titles.title: Provozní a analytická data z pilotního ověření hybridní technologie pro odstranění léčiv a mikropolutantů z odpadních vod zdravotnických zařízení

additional_descriptions.description:
Datová sada obsahuje provozní, analytická a experimentální data získaná během monitoringu a pilotního testování hybridní technologie pro odstranění léčiv a dalších mikropolutantů z odpadních vod produkovaných zdravotnickými zařízeními. Data pocházejí z reálného provozu dvou komunálních čistíren odpadních vod (ČOV A a ČOV B) a z poloprovozního zařízení instalovaného na jejich odtoku.

Soubor zahrnuje časové řady základních ukazatelů kvality vody (CHSK, BSK₅, NL, N-NH₄, Ncelk, Pcelk), koncentrace vybraných farmak a provozní parametry technologie (průtoky, dávkování H₂O₂, hydraulické zatížení, doby zdržení). Dále obsahuje data z jednotlivých stupňů technologie (filtrace, UV/H₂O₂, deperoxidace, MBBR, sorpce) umožňující vyhodnocení účinnosti jednotlivých kroků i celého systému.

Dataset umožňuje analýzu účinnosti odstraňování mikropolutantů, hodnocení provozní stability technologie a porovnání mezi různými provozními režimy a lokalitami.

Součástí je také souhrnná výzkumná zpráva hodnotící naměřená data.

Data byla získána kombinací dlouhodobého monitoringu ČOV a poloprovozních experimentů. Monitoring zahrnoval pravidelné odběry vzorků na přítoku a odtoku ČOV a sledování základních provozních parametrů. Na výstupu z biologického stupně byla instalována pilotní hybridní technologie zahrnující: mechanickou filtraci (pískový filtr), pokročilou oxidaci (UV/H₂O₂), odstranění zbytkového H₂O₂ (MnO₂ filtr), biologický stupeň (MBBR), finální sorpci (GAC nebo alternativní sorbenty).

Vzorky byly odebírány na jednotlivých technologických stupních a analyzovány pomocí HPLC-MS/MS pro stanovení koncentrací farmak. Současně byly sledovány standardní parametry kvality vody (CHSK, BSK₅, NL, N-NH₄, Ncelk, Pcelk).

Provozní data (průtok, zatížení, dávkování chemikálií) byla zaznamenávána kontinuálně nebo v definovaných intervalech.

Datová sada je poskytována jako kombinovaný soubor obsahující strukturovaná numerická data ve formátu Microsoft Excel (.xlsx) a doprovodné dokumenty ve formátu PDF (.pdf), které slouží k interpretaci a kontextualizaci těchto dat. Excelový soubor obsahuje primární experimentální a provozní data, zatímco PDF přílohy zahrnují rozšiřující informace, zejména grafická znázornění, tabulkové přehledy, popisy experimentálních podmínek a interpretace výsledků.

Numerická část datasetu (.xlsx) je členěna do tematických listů odpovídajících jednotlivým částem výzkumu, zejména monitoringu lokalit ČOV A a ČOV B a pilotního testování hybridní technologie. Obsahuje časově orientovaná data zahrnující základní ukazatele kvality vody (např. CHSK, BSK₅, NL, N-NH₄, Ncelk, Pcelk), koncentrace vybraných mikropolutantů stanovovaných metodou HPLC-MS/MS a provozní parametry technologie (např. průtoky, hydraulické zatížení, dávkování H₂O₂, doby zdržení, pH, teplota, rozpuštěný kyslík). Součástí jsou také vypočtené účinnosti odstranění jednotlivých ukazatelů.

Doprovodné PDF soubory obsahují podrobné výstupy z monitoringu a pilotního ověřování, včetně grafů časových řad, srovnání vstupních a výstupních koncentrací, technologických schémat, popisů experimentálních uspořádání a komentovaných výsledků. Tyto dokumenty umožňují správnou interpretaci dat, zejména ve vztahu k provozním podmínkám, změnám technologie a identifikovaným nestabilitám systému.

Data jsou uváděna v běžně používaných jednotkách (mg·l⁻¹, ng·l⁻¹, m³·d⁻¹, L·h⁻¹). Chybějící hodnoty jsou označeny jako "NA" a hodnoty pod mezí detekce jako "<LOD". Dataset zahrnuje reálná provozní data, která mohou vykazovat variabilitu způsobenou kolísáním zatížení čistíren odpadních vod a změnami provozních podmínek během experimentů.

Datová sada je určena pro další výzkumné a aplikační využití, zejména pro analýzu účinnosti hybridních technologií, modelování procesů čištění odpadních vod a validaci postupů pro odstraňování mikropolutantů. Kombinace strukturovaných dat a doprovodných dokumentů umožňuje reprodukovatelnost experimentů a správnou interpretaci výsledků.

metadata.title: J. Heyrovsky Institute Community
Experimental data for Ion-Induced Fragmentation of the FIBID precursor Iron Tetracarbonyl Acrolein
metadata.publisher: Ústav fyzikální chemie J. Heyrovského AV ČR

additional_descriptions.description: This dataset comprises experimental data on ion-induced fragmentation of iron tetracarbonyl acrolein, a potential precursor for focused ion and electron beam–induced deposition (FIBID/FEBID). The experiments were conducted using the COLIMACON setup at the ARIBE facility, GANIL (Caen, France), and the data analysis was carried out at the J. Heyrovský Institute of Physical Chemistry, Prague, Czech Republic.

The COLIMACON set-up employs a Wiley–McLaren type mass spectrometer in a cross-beam configuration. Fe(CO)4(acrolein) was introduced into the interaction zone as an effusive molecular beam. The vapour was sourced from a temperature-controlled glass reservoir (293 K) and delivered via a heated metal capillary (323 K). Prior to data collection, the sample was degassed to remove volatile impurities. This molecular beam, the projectile ion beam, and the mass spectrometer were aligned in a mutually orthogonal geometry, with their intersection defining the interaction region. The resulting fragments were detected according to their mass/charge ratio, which is related to their time of flight.

This dataset is organised into two primary directories: one containing raw experimental data and the other containing processed files for plotting mass spectra.

Folder 1: LST_files (Raw Data), this folder contains raw data in .lst format. These files were processed using ROOT to generate the subsequent text files. The mapping for the raw files is as follows: (1)240918_11.lst: Ne⁺ at 6 keV; (2) 240917_01.lst: Ne⁴⁺ at 40 keV; (3) 240916_03.lst: He⁺ at 16 keV. The Folder 2: Data_for_mass_spectra (Processed Data), folder contains the analysed .txt files and a Python script used to generate the mass spectra plots. The files are categorised by projectile type and kinetic energy: (1)  He_p_16keV.txt: Data for He⁺ projectile at 16 keV; (2)  Ne_4p_40keV.txt: Data for Ne⁴⁺ projectile at 40 keV, and (3) Ne_p_6keV.txt: Data for Ne⁺ projectile at 6 keV.

metadata.title: Dataset for Social predictors of depression in older adults – a cross-sectional study
metadata.publisher: Jihočeská univerzita v Českých Budějovicích

additional_descriptions.description: Depressive symptoms represent a significant health problem among older adults and are influenced by a range of sociodemographic and psychosocial factors. The aim of this study was to analyze the relationship between selected psychosocial and sociodemographic characteristics and the occurrence of depressive symptoms in individuals aged 65 years and older. The findings highlight the importance of sociodemographic and psychosocial factors in identifying older adults at risk of depressive symptoms. From a clinical perspective, the results underscore the need for targeted screening and preventive interventions, particularly among socially isolated and oldest-old individuals.

Methods: It was a descriptive cross-sectional study. Depressive symptoms were assessed using the Geriatric Depression Scale (GDS-15), which is a validated screening tool designed for the elderly population. Statistical analysis was performed using the chi-square test and Cramér's V to assess the strength of associations between variables. The research sample consisted of 403 individuals aged 65 years and older, selected through quota sampling. The sample was representative of the population of the South Bohemian Region of the Czech Republic in terms of gender and age.
descriptive statistics and statistical data analysis in file MS Xcel

metadata.title: Dataset for Use of social services among seniors 65 years and older in the South Bohemian Region: A quantitative cross-sectional study
metadata.publisher: Jihočeská univerzita v Českých Budějovicích

additional_descriptions.description: An aging population places a burden on the health and social systems. For regional care planning, it is necessary to know the extent of the use of social support and the factors associated with it. Most seniors in the South Bohemian Region do not use social services or counseling despite the available options. It is necessary to increase awareness and targeted support for the oldest and most socially isolated people.

Methodology: Quantitative descriptive cross-sectional survey. The sample consisted of 403 seniors (aged 65+) selected by quota according to gender, age, and region; without signs of cognitive impairment (MMSE). The analysis focused on seniors who live in ordinary households (n=354). Data were obtained through structured interviews in April 2024.

descriptive statistics and statistical data analysis (results)
The data is stored in a compressed file in ZIP format, this file, after extraction, contains MS Word, MS Excel and HTML files.

metadata.title: J. Heyrovsky Institute Community
Experimental and theoretical data for article Cluster Anions of Hydrated Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons: “Magic” Water Tetramer
metadata.publisher: Ústav fyzikální chemie J. Heyrovského AV ČR

additional_descriptions.description: Uploaded files contain negative and positive ion mass spectra of hydrated clusters of naphthalene, 1-cyanonaphthalene, phenanthrene and anthracene; and optimized geometries of mixed naphthalene-water clusters (1-2 Np, 0-9 W for neutrals and 1 Np, 7-9 W for anions) appearing in the article and its supplementary information. The ab-initio calculations complement the experimental data and help further interpretation of the mass spectra.

In the experiment, cluster beam was formed in supersonic expansion. Beam was then ionized by electron ionization or electron attachment and mass spectra of ionized beam were recorded using reflectron time-of-flight mass spectrometer. For calculations, different structures of Np1,2 (H2O)0-9 were optimized at the wB97XD/aug-cc-pvdz level of theory in the Gaussian 16 package. Initial guesses were based on already published structures of other hydrated PAH structures.

Folder Exp_MS contains data for the mass spectra in three file types: ascii with extension .asc - mass spectrum with mass calibration constants and spectrum resolution as a header; binary with extension .tfb - raw spectrum in time domain; text with extension .tfa - parameters of the detector. The files are contained in subfolders according to the date of experiment. An Excell spreadsheet with assignment of the spectra to article/SI figures is included. Folder Geoms contains coordinates in xyz format. The file names correspond to the structures shown in the Supplementary Information of the article.

metadata.title: J. Heyrovsky Institute Community
Data for Flexible Framework for Surface Hopping: From Hybrid Schemes for Machine Learning to Benchmarkable Nonadiabatic Dynamics
metadata.publisher: Ústav fyzikální chemie J. Heyrovského AV ČR

additional_descriptions.description:
Nonadiabatic molecular dynamics is a key technique for investigating a 
broad range of photochemical and photophysical processes. Among the 
established approaches, surface hopping schemes are widely used and can 
be easily integrated with various quantum chemistry programs or machine 
learning models. A flexible framework in MLatom includes
 a newly implemented Tully's fewest-switches surface hopping algorithm 
and its time-dependent Baeck-An variant. The capabilities of this 
framework are demonstrated through three representative examples 
corresponding to typical stages of a surface hopping study; which collectively constitute a curated dataset shared here. This framework enables accelerated development of machine 
learning models and provides deeper insight into nonadiabatic dynamics. 
It is available as a part of the open-source MLatom package.
The methodology is based on standard computational chemistry recipes and implementation using Python 3.
This data set contains scripts to run NAMD simulations, resulting trajectories in H5MD format and scripts analyzing them.

metadata.title: J. Heyrovsky Institute Community
Dataset for the article: Spatial Dynamics of the Fermi Level in Electrolyte-Gated Graphene
metadata.publisher: Ústav fyzikální chemie J. Heyrovského AV ČR

additional_descriptions.description: This data supports a study investigating how electric fields propagate in graphene, which is essential for optimizing their performance in electronic, energy, and sensing devices that require precise control of charge carrier density. The dataset captures Fermi level dynamics in monolayer graphene obtained using in situ Raman spectroscopy combined with local voltage application via an electrolyte microdroplet. We observe a sharp initial shift of the Fermi level towards the charge-neutral Dirac point when crossing from the biased microdroplet interface into the adjacent unbiased graphene, followed by a gradual equilibration extending tens of micrometers away. Notably, the Fermi level does not fully recover to its undoped state within this range. We attribute these long-range effects to the intrinsically low density of states of graphene, which limits its ability to screen the electric field, allowing the potential to decay gradually beyond the biased region.
Raman spectroscopy, optical microscopy, In situ Microdroplet Raman Spectroelectrochemistry.
The data are provided in .png, .text, .dat, .xlsx, or .LabSpec6Spectrum files. All the datasets are located in folders named after the figures (eg, Figure 1, Figure 2, etc.).

metadata.title: Dataset for: Chemical Accuracy in Computational Prediction of PFAS-Cyclodextrin Binding: A Multi-Level SQM/QM Study with Experimental Validation
metadata.publisher: Technická univerzita v Liberci

additional_descriptions.description: This data support article: Chemical Accuracy in Computational Prediction of PFAS-Cyclodextrin Binding: A Multi-Level SQM/QM Study with Experimental Validation.

Isothermal Titration Calorimetry (ITC) measurements were undertaken on a Low Volume (LV) Nano ITC (601000, TA Instruments) at 298.15 K, with experimental conditions outlined in Table 1. In general, 1mL of the appropriate PFAS and CD samples were firstly de-gassed via sonication for 30 mins before being used in measurements, with a mixing speed of 200 RPM, 300 s intervals, and an active cell volume of 165 μL. Each sample had the appropriate background heat changes (mixing/solvation energies) of PFAS to water titrations measured and subsequently subtracted from the CD complexation titrations.
Data structure: PF4a.xls; PF5a.xls; PF6a.xls; PF7a.xls; PF4a.xls

metadata.title: The list of differentially expressed genes in Langerhans Cells and Macrophages During Early Tick-Borne Encephalitis Virus Infection (dataset)
metadata.publisher: Jihočeská univerzita v Českých Budějovicích

additional_descriptions.description: The dataset presents differentially expressed genes in human Langerhans cells and Macrophages during early stages of Tick-Borne Encephalitis Virus infection

The list of differentially expressed genes from each pairwise comparison is based on FPKM values cross-sample normalised by geometric normalisation as implemented by Cufflinks and Cuffdiff. These normalised counts were calculated at 5 % significance level with FDR to control for false positive observations. All significant and non-significant differentially expressed genes are presented in this dataset.

The dataset is a tab-separated value (tsv) file and contains the list of differentially methylated genes. The table contains 14 columns: test_id, gene_id, gene locus,  sample_1, sample_2, status, value_1, value_2, log2(fold_change), test_stat, p_value, q_value, significant, and 2810809 rows, where each row represents one annotated human genomic region. The details of the dataset structure are covered in the Cuffdiff documentation (https://cole-trapnell-lab.github.io/cufflinks/cuffdiff/).

metadata.title: Dataset z monitoringu veřejného plaveckého bazénu a poloprovozní technologie na recyklaci vody z praní pískových filtrů z hlediska výskytu vedlejších produktů dezinfekce (DBPs) a dalších látek
metadata.publisher: Technická univerzita v Liberci

additional_descriptions.description: Dataset je výstupem řešení dvouletého projektu zaměřeného na výzkum a vývoj v oblasti recyklačních technologií pracích vod z pískových filtrů v plaveckých bazénech s důrazem na problematiku možné kumulace vedlejších produktů dezinfekce (DBPs), které mohou představovat potenciální zdravotní riziko. První část dat představuje výsledky monitoringu veřejného plaveckého bazénu o objemu 750 m3 bez instalované recyklační technologie. Druhá část dat je výstupem monitoringu poloprovozní recyklační technologie pracích vod z pískových filtrů, která byla na shodném bazéně provozovaná po dobu více než 8 měsíců. Základním inovativním prvkem technologie byl adsorpčně-katalytický filtr s materiálem na bázi uhlíkových nanotrubic (CNT). Před ním byl předřazen filtr se zeolitovou náplní a mechanické svíčkové filtry, za adsorpčně-katalytickým filtrem následovala jednotka reverzní osmózy a UVC lampa. Technologie byla provozována ve třech různých fázích (1-3), které se lišily použitým CNT materiálem (Fáze 1 – CNT modifikované sírou; Fáze 3 – CNT bez modifikace) a zařazením dávkování persíranu sodného (Fáze 2 – CNT modifikované sírou s dávkováním persíranu sodného).

Sledovanými parametry v odebíraných vzorcích vod byly: teplota, pH, volný a vázaný chlor, trihalomethany (THM), dichloracetonitril (DCAN), chloralhydrát, celkový organický uhlík, celkový dusík, amoniakální dusík, KTJ 36 °C, KTJ 22 °C. V rámci monitoringu bazénu byl mikrobiologický monitoring rozšířen o stanovení Escherichia coli a koliformní bakterie, enterokoky, Clostridium perfringens a Pseudomonas aeruginosa. Volný a vázaný chlor byl měřen na místě pomocí kolorimetru Hach (DPD metoda), pH a teplota pomocí přenosného multimetru Hach, stanovení TOC/TN na analyzátoru Jena, stanovení amoniakálního dusíku kyvetovým testem Hach LCK 304, stanovení THM na GC-MS/ECD (Thermo Scientific), stanovení DCAN a chloralhydrátu na GC-MS (zavedena nová metoda). V rámci mikrobiologických analýz byly použity metody: ČSN EN ISO 7899-2, SOP Stanovení Clostridium perfringens ve vodách (Postup uveden vyhláškou MZ č. 252/2004 Sb. v platném znění), ČSN EN ISO 9308-1, ČSN EN ISO 16266, ČSN EN ISO 6222.

Data ve formátu .xlsx (Microsoft Excel) zahrnují data získaná v průběhu let 2024 a 2025 v městském plaveckém bazéně v rámci monitoringu výskytu DBPs (záložka "bazén data") a v rámci monitoringu nainstalované poloprovozní technologie na recyklaci pracích vod z pískových filtrů (záložky "poloprovoz data"; "poloprovoz grafy" FÁZE 1 – 3). Data jsou převedena i do formátu .csv (Popis_vzorku.csv; Bazen_data.csv; Poloprovoz_data.csv). V souboru Graficke_zpracovani_dat_poloprovoz.pdf jsou uvedeny grafy k datům získaným v průběhu poloprovozu. V souboru Fotodokumentace_poloprovozni_technologie.pdf je obrazová dokumentace technologie i s popisem jednotlivých odběrových míst.

metadata.title: Aerační potenciál nádrží pro řízenou aeraci vodních toků - datová sada
metadata.publisher: České vysoké učení technické v Praze

additional_titles.title: Aeration potential of reservoirs for controlled aeration of streams - dataset

additional_descriptions.description: Datová sada "Aerační potenciál nádrží pro řízenou aeraci vodních toků" obsahující data vodních toků a vodních děl v České republice popisující ohroženost vodních toků nedostatkem kyslíku s potenciálem jeho zvýšením na ohrožených vodních tocích po vybranými nádržemi. Sada obsahuje 2 geografické datové vrstvy a tabulku aeračního potenciálu aerátoru s prstencovým vodním skokem. Geografické vrstvy jsou linie úseků vodních toků s informacemi o jejich ohroženosti nedostatkem kyslíku a body vodních děl (nádrže a významné rybníky), které mají potenciál vhodným způsobem zvyšovat obsah rozpuštěného kyslíku na odtoku z vodního díla. Data jsou uložena ve formátu ShapeFile spol. ESRI a MS Excel.

The dataset "Aeration potential of reservoirs for controlled aeration of watercourses" containing data on watercourses and waterworks in the Czech Republic, describing the vulnerability of watercourses to oxygen deficiency with the potential for its increase in endangered watercourses after selected reservoirs. The set contains two geographic data layers and a table of the aeration potential of an aerator with a ring waterfall. The geographic layers are lines of watercourse sections with information on their vulnerability to oxygen deficiency and points of waterworks (reservoirs and significant ponds) that have the potential to increase the dissolved oxygen content at the outlet of the waterworks in an appropriate manner. The data are stored in ESRI ShapeFile and MS Excel formats.

Data vznikla jako výsledek původního výzkumu zpracovatele. Hlavním podkladem byly informace o vodních tocích v ČR obsažené v Plánech hlavních povodí Labe, Dunaje a Odry, kde jsou mmojiné i informace o tocích vykazující problémy s nedostatkem rozpuštěného kyslíku ve vodě. Na to navazoval výzkum nádrží, které mají potenciál ohrožené vodní toky okysličit. Datová sada popisující přínos aerátoru s prstencovým vodním skokem vznikla na základě laboratorních měření a měření In Situ s aerátorem na několika lokalitách v ČR v průběhu roku 2025.

The data was created as a result of the author's original research. The main source was information on watercourses in the Czech Republic contained in the Plans for the Main River Basins of the Elbe, Danube, and Odra, which also include information on watercourses with problems of dissolved oxygen deficiency in water. This was followed by research on reservoirs that have the potential to oxygenate endangered watercourses. The dataset describing the contribution of the ring hydraulic jump aerator was created on the basis of laboratory measurements and in situ measurements with the aerator at several locations in the Czech Republic during 2025.
Datové geografické sady jsou ve formátu Shapefile spol. ESRI. Jedná se o bodová a liiává data ve 2D reprezentující významné vodní toky a body v místech hrází vodních nádrží.
The geographic data sets are in ESRI Shapefile format. These are point and line data in 2D representing significant watercourses and points at the locations of water reservoir dams.

metadata.title: Dataset k projektu 'Vývoj akreditovaných metodik pro hodnocení kvality vody prostřednictvím molekulárně-genetických metod'
metadata.publisher: Technická univerzita v Liberci

additional_descriptions.description: Dataset obsahuje surová data z molekulárně-genetických analýz (qPCR a NGS) naměřená během řešení projektu "Vývoj akreditovaných metodik pro hodnocení kvality vody prostřednictvím molekulárně-genetických metod". Cílem projektu bylo vytvořit akreditované metodiky pro hodnocení mikrobiální kvality vod prostřednictvím molekulárně-genetických analýz a pro hodnocení mikrobiálního biofilmu ve vodohospodářských stavbách pomocí nanovlákenných nosičů biomasy a standardních mikrobiologických a molekulárně-genetických analýz. Dataset obsahuje osvědčení o akreditaci metodik "Stanovení indikátorových mikroorganismů pomocí qPCR analýzy" (interní označení SOP M 10) a "Stanovení celkového bakteriálního oživení a indikátorových mikroorganismů pomocí qPCR analýzy" (interní označení SOP M 11) vydané Českým institutem pro akreditaci, o.p.s. V datasetu jsou dále zahrnuty zprávy o verifikaci detekce indikátorových mikroorganismů molekulárně-genetickými analýzami (testy opakovatelnosti), o mezilaboratorní porovnávací zkoušce detekce indikátorových mikroorganismů pomocí qPCR analýzy, o verifikaci stanovení celkového bakteriálního oživení a indikátorových mikroorganismů v biofilmu pomocí qPCR analýzy (testy opakovatelnosti) a o mezilaboratorní porovnávací zkoušce detekce indikátorových mikroorganismů na nosičích biomasy pomocí qPCR analýzy. V datasetu se dále nachází fotodokumentace k nanovlákenným nosičům, jako jsou technická schémata a fotografie nosiče, snímky nanovlákenné vrstvy nosičů z výroby, po sterilizaci a po umístění ve vodohospodářských stavbách, pořízené elektronovým rastrovacím a optickým mikroskopem. Součástí datasetu jsou rovněž sekvence qPCR standardů k jednotlivým primerům.

Metodologie zahrnovala vývoj, optimalizaci a validaci molekulárně-genetických (qPCR) metod pro detekci indikátorových mikroorganismů ve vodě a v mikrobiálním biofilmu. Mezi sledované indikátorové mikroorganismy patřily koliformní bakterie, Escherichia coli, enterokoky, Clostridium perfringens, Staphylococcus aureus, Pseudomonas aeruginosa a Legionella spp.

V první fázi byl vytvořen protokol zpracování vzorků vody zahrnující odběr vzorků, membránovou filtraci a izolaci DNA. Současně byly vybrány, testovány a optimalizovány qPCR primery a qPCR podmínky a byla stanovena kritéria pro potvrzení přítomnosti indikátorových mikroorganismů ve vzorcích. Metodika byla průběžně upravována na základě výsledků monitoringu kvality pitné, rekreační (bazénové) a teplé vody prováděného kombinací molekulárně-genetických metod (qPCR) a standardního mikrobiologického rozboru (kultivace na chromogenním médiu). Součástí metodologického postupu bylo stanovení detekčního limitu qPCR analýzy pomocí ředících řad čistých kultur indikátorových mikroorganismů.

Pro hodnocení mikrobiálního biofilmu byly vyvíjeny a testovány různé varianty nanovlákenných nosičů, zahrnující dvě formy upořádání nanovláken (plošná a na přízi) a nanovlákna z různých polymerů (polyamid, polyuretan a polyvinylidenfluorid). Ve všech případech byla nanovlákna vyrobena technologií střídavého elektrického zvlákňování. Jednotlivé varianty byly charakterizovány mikroskopickými metodami, konkrétně optickou a rastrovací elektronovou mikroskopií. Paralelně probíhal vývoj uspořádání nanovlákenného nosiče, ochranného obalu a způsobu fixace nanovlákenného nosiče k podpůrné konstrukci. Varianty nanovlákenných nosičů a podpůrných konstrukcí s ochranným obalem byly testovány v laboratorním prostředí a během monitoringu biofilmu ve vodohospodářských stavbách. Hodnocení variant probíhalo na základě výsledků qPCR analýzy, mikroskopické analýzy a manipulaci vzorků při vzorkování.

U vybraných vzorků vody a biofilmu odebraných během monitoringu byla spolu s pozitivními kontrolami provedena identifikace jejich mikrobiálního složení pomocí NGS analýzy

Validace obou metodik zahrnovala testy opakovatelnosti a mezilaboratorní porovnávací zkoušky provedené na vzorcích vody a biofilmu na nosičích odebraných z vodohospodářských staveb a na vzorcích vody a biofilmu na nosičích uměle kontaminovaných indikátorovými mikroorganismy.
V zazipovaném souboru je REDME soubor obsahující informace o obsahu zazipovaného souboru.

metadata.title: Data set for Synthesis and Biological Activity of 2,6-Disubstituted and 2,6,9-Trisubstituted 7-deazapurine Nucleobases
metadata.publisher: Ústav organické chemie a biochemie AV ČR

additional_descriptions.description: High-throughput screening of IMTM compound library for modulatory activity on adenosine receptors identified two deazapurine hits based on 2,6-diaryl-7-deazapurines. In this study, a series of 16 new 2,6-diaryl-7-dezapurine nucleobases together with four N-9 substituted derivatives were synthesized. The synthesis was based on chemoselective Suzuki cross-coupling into position 6 followed by second Suzuki cross-coupling at position 2 under harsher conditions. Both cross-couplings were performed on N-9 protected nucleobases. Biologial activity profiling identified several potent antagonists of all four subtypes of adenosine receptors and four compounds as sub-micromolar and very selective antagonists of A3. Several final compounds showed potent in vitro cytotoxic activity on a small panel of cancer cell lines, but with no selectivity against non-malignant fibroblasts BJ and MRC-5.
Data set contains raw NMR data of all new compounds and UPLC chromatograms of all final compounds

metadata.title: Data on Nurse-Led Nutrition Education in Secondary Prevention of Stable Coronary Artery Disease: Cross-Sectional Study
metadata.publisher: Jihočeská univerzita v Českých Budějovicích

additional_descriptions.description:
Background
Nutrition education is a key component of secondary prevention in patients with stable coronary artery disease (SCAD). Although clinical guidelines emphasise lifestyle counselling, limited attention has been paid to how nutrition education is organised and who delivers it in inpatient settings.
Aim
To describe current practices in nutrition education for patients with SCAD in inpatient care and to identify organisational and professional factors associated with nurse-led nutrition education.
Results
Nurses were the most frequently reported providers of nutrition education. Among respondents, 36.4% reported that nurses always provided nutrition education, and 44.5% reported that nurses often provided it. The frequency of education differed significantly across professional groups (χ² = 37.16, p = 0.042; Cramer's V = 0.25). Nurse-led nutrition education was more commonly reported in cardiology-focused wards and among nurses with greater professional experience. Limited time allocation for nutrition education was frequently reported across settings. In multivariable analysis, workplace context and professional experience were independently associated with nurse-led nutrition education, whereas hospital location was not.
Conclusions
Nurses play a central role in delivering nutrition education to patients with SCAD in inpatient care. The findings highlight that nurse-led nutrition education is strongly influenced by organisational conditions rather than individual characteristics alone, underscoring the need for institutional support to facilitate consistent implementation of guideline-based lifestyle counselling.

A cross-sectional survey was conducted among 602 nurses working in inpatient cardiology, internal medicine, and cardiac surgery wards. The primary outcome was nurse-led nutrition education, defined as cases in which nurses were identified as the primary providers of nutrition education. Associations were analysed using descriptive statistics, chi-square tests, and logistic regression.

metadata.title: Data on Validation of the HLS-Child-Q15 questionnaire in older school-aged children (9–10 years) in the Czech Republic
metadata.publisher: Jihočeská univerzita v Českých Budějovicích

additional_descriptions.description:
Aim
The aim of this study was to evaluate the psychometric properties of the Czech version of the HLS-Child-Q15 questionnaire in older school-age children (9–10 years old) in the Czech Republic.
Background Currently, there is no Czech translation of the HLS-Child-Q15 (or any other tool) in the Czech language for assessing children's health literacy. 
Design: A cross-sectional validation study.
Results
The internal consistency of the health literacy scale reaches a value of 0.800 (95% CI = 0.774-0.823), and split-half reliability, measured by the Spearman-Brown coefficient (r = 0.788), indicates relatively high internal consistency. The test-retest confirmed a high degree of internal consistency in all three regions tested, with inter-item correlations ranging from 0.003 to 0.503. Tukey's test does not suggest any undesirable additional additivity in any of the regions studied. Criterion validity was tested using the Chi-square test and Cramer's V measure of association. A statistically significant correlation was demonstrated for all relationships tested.
Conclusion
The Czech version of the HLS-Child-Q15 is a valid and reliable tool for assessing health literacy in older school-aged children (9–10 years) in the Czech Republic. This questionnaire demonstrates high internal consistency and can be used to assess the health literacy of school-aged children (9–10 years old) in the Czech Republic.

The sample consisted of 1,333 elementary school students from three regions of the Czech Republic, all of whom were 9–10 years old at the time of the study. Psychometric properties included Cronbach's alpha for internal consistency, content validity, construct validity, criterion validity, and factor analysis.

metadata.title: Data on Health Literacy among Older School-Aged Children (9–10 years) in the Czech Republic – cross-sectional study
metadata.publisher: Jihočeská univerzita v Českých Budějovicích

additional_descriptions.description:
Background: Health literacy is currently a very topical issue worldwide. Health literacy is essential for adults, children, and adolescents, who are increasingly making independent decisions about their own health.

Aim: This study aimed to assess the health literacy of school-aged children (9-10 years old) in the areas of healthcare, health promotion, and disease prevention.

Results: Health literacy among older school-age children in the Czech Republic is average. Overall health literacy (HL) consists of three basic domains: a) HC (health care), b) DP (disease prevention), and c) HP (health promotion). The questionnaire covers various health-related topics, such as "health-related communication," "nutrition," "health care," and "treatment adherence."   The lowest average scores are in the Health Care domain (3.2 ± 0.4), while the highest scores are in the Health Promotion domain (3.4 ± 0.5). Testing the overall health literacy scale about gender and year of birth showed no statistically significant differences between boys and girls.

Conclusion: The survey results indicated the average level of health literacy knowledge among children aged 9-10 years. No statistically significant differences were found between the knowledge of girls and boys. Promoting health literacy (HL) at an early age can lay the foundation for overall health, quality of life, and health literacy throughout life. The findings align with the current state of society and its approach to health, highlighting the need to enhance health literacy among children from the youngest age groups.

A quantitative research design was employed for this research study. It was a multicenter cross-sectional study. The HLS-Child-Q15 questionnaire was used. The research sample consisted of fourth-grade elementary school students aged 9 and 10.  It was a quota selection. A total of 1,333 pupils were included in the research study.

Dataset for the article Increase in testosterone and cortisol one week after repeated exercise in a cold environment
metadata.publisher: Univerzita Karlova

additional_descriptions.description: The datasets contain the results of measurements of a group of 12 probands (men) before 5 days of outdoor physical training (2-3 °C), immediately after it, and after a week of recovery. They include results of steroid levels, body composition, and other biochemical parameters.

The dataset include resutls of measurements of a group of healthy male athletes (n = 12, aged 20.5 ± 1 year, height 181 ± 7.7 cm) exposed to 5 days of outdoor physical training (2 °C–3 °C) with increasing intensity of exercise and cold exposure. Venous blood was collected, and body bioelectrical impedance was measured before and after the 5-day experiment, and after a 7-day recovery. Circulating levels of testosterone, cortisol, androstenedione, dehydroepiandrosterone sulphate, 17-hydroxyprogesterone, calcifediol, interleukin-6, C-reactive protein, and erythrocyte superoxide dismutase activity were analysed.

The experimental protocol was conducted in winter in a mountain environment at 1,000 m a.s.l. (Giant Mountain National Park, Czechia). Baseline blood sampling and body-composition measurements were performed on site under identical ambient conditions. All participants completed a 5-day structured program of combined physical exercise and cold exposure (EC) followed by 7 days of recovery under standard environmental conditions (ECR).
Body composition was assessed using the InBody 770.
Two samples of whole peripheral venous blood were collected by a trained medical professional into serum (with micronised silicate particles, no anticoagulant) and plasma (treated with heparin) test tubes (VACUETTE®). After 15 min, the whole and clotted blood were centrifuged (2,000 × g for 15 min). Plasma and serum supernatants were then aliquoted and frozen to −80 °C until further analysis.
Plasma interleukin-6 (IL-6) was determined by chemiluminescence immunoassay (CLIA) using an automated analyser DXI Beckman Coulter (IL-6 reference values <6.4 ng/L). Serum C-reactive protein (CRP) was determined by the immunoturbidimetric method using an automated analyser DXI Beckman Coulter (CRP reference values <0.5 mg/L).
Collected serum samples were used for the detection of hormones using liquid chromatography tandem-mass spectrometry (LC-MS/MS) methods. The analysis of testosterone, cortisol, androstenedione, DHEA-S, and 17(OH)Progesterone was performed using IVD-certified ClinMass® LC-MS/MS Complete Kit Steroids in Serum/Plasma from RECIPE (RECIPE Chemicals + Instruments GmbH, München, Germany). Calcifediol was measured according to Bičíková et al. (2022).
Erythrocytes separated from clotted blood were lysed in four times their volume of ice-cold HPLC-grade water and centrifuged at 10,000 × g for 15 min at 4 °C. The supernatant (erythrocyte lysate) was then analysed using a Superoxide Dismutase ELISA kit (Cayman Chemical, 706002) following the manufacturer's instructions.

metadata.title: Dataset pocházející z laboratorních a pilotních experimentů s cílem ověřit hybridní technologii pro eliminaci mikropolutantů v odpadních vodách
metadata.publisher: Technická univerzita v Liberci

additional_descriptions.description:
V rámci výzkumu byly získány rozsáhlé datasety zaměřené na koncentrace vybraných mikropolutantů (např. ibuprofen, diclofenac, karbamazepin, venlafaxin a další) v odpadních vodách před a po aplikaci jednotlivých stupňů hybridní technologie. Data byla získávána v průběhu dvou let z laboratorních testů a provozováním pilotní jednotky instalované na výusti ČOV přijímající vody ze zdravotnického zařízení. Celkem bylo analyzováno přes 150 vzorků, přičemž každý vzorek obsahoval data o minimálně 10–20 sledovaných látkách v různých částech technologie (nátok, za AOP, za MBBR, za sorpcí). Záznamy byly doplněny o podpůrné parametry (např. CHSK, TSS, pH, teplota, průtok) a provozní podmínky.

Data byla využita pro výpočet účinností jednotlivých technologických stupňů, porovnání vývoje koncentrací a hodnocení celkové efektivity systému. Součástí datového zpracování byly i analýzy trendů, identifikace variability a porovnání s referenčními zdroji. Výsledky datových analýz sloužily jako základ pro návrh optimalizací technologie a doporučení pro implementaci v různých provozních podmínkách. Veškerá data byla uchovávána ve standardizovaném formátu (Excel, CSV aj.), opatřena metadaty a zajištěna pro další využití a archivaci.

Metodologický přístup projektu ELEM vycházel z návrhu, realizace a ověření vícestupňové hybridní technologie, zaměřené na eliminaci farmaceuticky aktivních mikropolutantů z biologicky vyčištěné odpadní vody ze zdravotnických zařízení.vPostup zahrnoval následující kroky: 1. Technický návrh systému – sestavení kombinace technologických stupňů zahrnujících odstranění nerozpuštěných látek, dávkování oxidačního činidla (H₂O₂), UV lampu, reaktor s biofilmem (MBBR) a sorpční nádrž s výměnnou náplní. 2. Instalace a provoz pilotní jednotky – provedená na výusti ČOV přijímající vody ze zdravotnického zařízení; zahrnovala záběh systému, biologickou kolonizaci, kalibraci dávkování a provozní nastavení. 3.Vzorkování a analýzy – odběry v jednotlivých technologických uzlech, analýzy více než 20 cílových mikropolutantů (HPLC-MS/MS), sledování provozních a fyzikálně-chemických parametrů (např. CHSK, pH, TSS). 4.Vyhodnocení účinností – výpočty procentuální účinnosti pro jednotlivé látky a stupně, identifikace degradačních trendů, akumulace v sorbentech. 5. Technicko-ekonomické posouzení – výpočet provozních nákladů (OPEX), porovnání s jinými metodami (ozonizace, membránové systémy), návrh scénářů implementace a dalšího rozvoje.

ZIP Archiv obsahuje kompletní datové a dokumentační podklady k projektu SS06020091 – ELEM, zaměřenému na vývoj a ověření hybridní technologie pro odstranění farmaceuticky aktivních mikropolutantů z odpadních vod produkovaných zdravotnickými zařízeními. Hlavní části archivu: Experimentální data: Výsledky měření koncentrací mikropolutantů v jednotlivých technologických stupních (UV/H₂O₂, MBBR, sorpce) včetně podpůrných provozních parametrů (CHSK, TSS, pH apod.). Vyhodnocení a grafy: Přehledové tabulky a grafické výstupy znázorňující účinnosti odstranění látek a změny koncentrací v průběhu času. Technická dokumentace: Popis technologie, výstup ověřené technologie, průběžné a závěrečné zprávy projektu. Fotodokumentace: Fotografie z instalace, provozu a vzorkování na pilotní jednotce. Archiv je strukturován tak, aby byl přehledně využitelný pro odborné posouzení technologie, validaci dat a další aplikaci výsledků výzkumu.

metadata.title: Dataset pocházející z laboratorních a pilotních experimentů zaměřených na studium denitrifikace a post-denitrifikace
metadata.publisher: Technická univerzita v Liberci

additional_descriptions.description: Dataset pochází z laboratorních a pilotních experimentů zaměřených na studium procesů denitrifikace a post-denitrifikace v systémech biologického čištění odpadních vod. Data byla získána v rámci řízených experimentů realizovaných v laboratorním měřítku a následně ověřena v pilotním provozu, přičemž cílem bylo posoudit účinnost odstraňování dusíkatých látek, stabilitu procesu a chování denitrifikační biomasy za různých provozních podmínek. Dataset zahrnuje časové řady provozních a analytických parametrů, zejména koncentrace dusičnanového a dusitanového dusíku, amoniakálního dusíku, celkového dusíku, CHSK/N poměru, rozpuštěného kyslíku, pH, teploty a hydraulických zatížení. Součástí dat jsou rovněž informace o charakteru biomasy, jejím původu a způsobu separace, včetně sledování aktivity suspenzní denitrifikační populace pocházející z biofilmových systémů. Dataset umožňuje detailní analýzu kinetiky denitrifikace, porovnání laboratorních a pilotních výsledků a vyhodnocení potenciálu technologického uspořádání pro praktickou aplikaci v čistírnách odpadních vod. Data slouží jako podklad pro optimalizaci provozních parametrů, návrh technologických stupňů a hodnocení implementačního potenciálu ověřené technologie.

Experimentální data byla získána kombinací laboratorních a pilotních experimentů zaměřených na studium denitrifikace a post-denitrifikace v systémech biologického čištění odpadních vod. Laboratorní testy byly prováděny v řízených reaktorech s kontrolovanými provozními podmínkami, kde byla sledována aktivita denitrifikační biomasy při různých poměrech CHSK/N, hydraulickém zatížení, teplotě a koncentraci rozpuštěného kyslíku. Pilotní experimenty byly realizovány na technologickém uspořádání simulujícím reálný provoz čistírny odpadních vod, včetně biofilmových a suspenzních systémů. V průběhu experimentů byly pravidelně odebírány vzorky a analyzovány standardními analytickými metodami pro stanovení forem dusíku a základních provozních parametrů. Zásadní bylo měření koncentrace N2O pomocí speciální sondy. Získaná data byla vyhodnocována jako časové řady a použita pro porovnání laboratorních a pilotních výsledků, posouzení stability procesu a optimalizaci provozních podmínek.

ZIP soubor obsahuje strukturovaný dataset z laboratorních a pilotních experimentů zaměřených na denitrifikaci a post-denitrifikaci. Součástí jsou datové soubory s naměřenými provozními a analytickými parametry, časové řady sledovaných ukazatelů a přehledné tabulky pro vyhodnocení experimentů. Dále obsahuje doplňující soubory s popisem experimentálních podmínek, uspořádání testů a základními metadata k jednotlivým měřením. Data jsou uložena ve standardních formátech umožňujících další analýzu a opakované využití.

metadata.title: Dataset underlying the paper on Cytoplasmic Force Microscopy
metadata.publisher: Vysoké učení technické v Brně

additional_descriptions.description: This record contains the data underlying the article introducing Cytoplasmic Force Microscopy, a label-free method combining hiQPI and AID4CMF to quantify intracellular mass transfer and cytoplasmic mechanical forces in live cancer cells with attoNewton sensitivity.

Data acquisition was performed using Holographic Incoherent-light-source Quantitative Phase Imaging (hiQPI) on a Q-Phase microscope (TELIGHT Brno, Czech Republic). The raw holograms were subsequently processed using the SophiQ software from the same manufacturer to obtain quantitative phase images. All resulting quantitative phase data are provided in this dataset.

Pilot experiment. The pilot experiment includes two hiQPI datasets. The first dataset contains measurements of an HT1080 cell acquired with a 30-second time step, stored as compensated hiQPI phase images expressed in radians in TIFF format. The second dataset comprises measurements of a T15 cell acquired with a 1-second time step, also provided as compensated hiQPI phase images expressed in radians in TIFF format.  Comparative measurements of cytoplasmic mechanical forces. This section includes datasets used for the comparative analysis of cytoplasmic mechanical forces in different cell types. The first dataset contains measurements of a K2 cell acquired with time steps ranging from 0.26 to 10 seconds. The data are provided as compensated hiQPI phase images expressed in radians and stored in NumPy (.npy) format. For the 0.26-second time series, only the first 600 hiQPI images were used due to lower image quality. The second dataset comprises measurements of a T15 cell acquired with time steps ranging from 0.28 to 10 seconds. These data are likewise provided as compensated hiQPI phase images expressed in radians and stored in NumPy (.npy) format.

metadata.title: CAMx daily outputs of Temporal Source Apportionment  of PM2.5 over Central Europe 2010-2019 [dataset]
metadata.publisher: Univerzita Karlova

additional_descriptions.description: Fine particulate matter (PM2.5) pollution remains a critical health issue in Central Europe and concentrations frequently exceed WHO guidelines despite some improvements. While numerous studies have quantified the spatial and sectorial sources of urban PM as well as other factors such meteorological driver that contribute to adverse urban PM pollution, the temporal origin, i.e. how much of pollution originates from emissions from previous days has received minimum attention. To address this gap we developed a novel Temporal Source Apportionment approach within the CAMx chemical transport model, driven by WRF meteorology, to quantify the long-term average contributions of emissions from the preceding 14 days to daily average PM concentrations, focusing on the 2010-2019 period and Central Europe. The data published here contain the daily mean concentrations for PM2.5 and its components including the contributions from previous days emissions (up to 1 week), from boundary conditions and from biogenic emissions.

CAMx model applied over 2010-2019 at 9km x 9km centered over Prague. The model configuration is identical to one in Prieto Perez et al.(2025). See  PRIETO PEREZ, A.P., Huszár, P. and Karlický, J.: Validation of multi-model decadal simulations of present-day central European air-quality, Atmos. Environ., 349, 121077, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.atmosenv.2025.121077, 2025.
See the README.txt file.

metadata.title: J. Heyrovsky Institute Community
A New Hollow Fiber-Based Liquid-Phase Microextraction Method for the Determination of Antihypertensive Drug Lercanidipine in Biological Samples
metadata.publisher: Ústav fyzikální chemie J. Heyrovského AV ČR

additional_descriptions.description: The attached datasets include the data recorded by the determination of the antihypertensive drug lercandipine (LCN) in biological samples (blood plasma, serum, and urine) using combined hollow fiber liquid phase microextraction (HF-LPME) with square wave voltammetric determination on screen-printed diamond electrodes.

A hollow fiber-based liquid-phase microextraction (HF-LPME) method was used for the separation of disturbing compounds and for the preconcentration of antihypertensive drug lercandipine (LCN) from aqueous and biological samples. A new square-wave voltammetric method on diamond screen-printed electrodes was developed for LCN determination.

HF-LPME was combined with optimized square wave voltammetry (SWV) on a cathodically pre-treated screenprinted boron-doped diamond electrode (SP-BDDE). Optimum HF-LPME conditions were: supported liquid membrane (SLM) dodecane, 0.02 mol L−1 Britton-Robinson buffer (BRB, pH = 3) acceptor phase, BRB (pH = 7) donor phase, and time of extraction 30 min. Limits of quantification (LOQ) and detection (LOD) were 3.3 and 1.1 nmol L−1, respectively. The applicability of the developed method was verified on human urine, blood serum, and blood plasma with 20 and 100 nmol L−1 LCN addition.

metadata.title: J. Heyrovsky Institute Community
Experimental and theoretical data for article Negative Cluster Ions of Hydrated Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons: Evidence for “Magic” Water Tetramer
metadata.publisher: Ústav fyzikální chemie J. Heyrovského AV ČR

additional_descriptions.description:
Uploaded files contain negative and positive ion mass spectra of 
hydrated clusters of naphthalene, cyanonaphthalene, phenanthrene and 
anthracene; and optimized geometries of mixed naphthalene-water clusters
 (1-2 Np, 0-9 W) appearing in the article and its supplementary 
information. The ab-initio calculations complement the experimental data
 and help further interpretation of the mass spectra.

In the experiment, cluster beam was formed in supersonic expansion. Beam was then ionized by electron ionization or electron attachment and mass spectra of ionized beam were recorded using reflectron time-of-flight mass spectrometer.

For calculations, different structures of Np1,2 (H2O)0-9 were optimized at the wB97XD/aug-cc-pvdz level of theory in the Gaussian 16 package. Initial guesses were based on already published structures of other hydrated PAH structures.

Folder Exp_MS contains data for the mass spectra in three file types: ascii with extension .asc - mass spectrum with mass calibration constants and spectrum resolution as a header; binary with extension .tfb - raw spectrum in time domain; text with extension .tfa - parameters of the detector. The files are contained in subfolders according to the date of experiment. An Excell spreadsheet with assignment of the spectra to article/SI figures is included

Folder Geoms contains coordinates in xyz format. The file names correspond to the structures shown in the Supplementary Information of the article.
